commit.message:
Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix heartbeat pulse saturating keyboard to pink

Rings decayed too slowly and accumulated, washing out the whole keyboard.
Tightened ring width (0.8 -> 0.5), faster decay (3.5x vs 1.2x), shorter
ring lifetime (2s vs 4s), and slower expansion. Beats now pulse clearly
with the keyboard returning to dark between beats.

commit.message:
fix(bom-upload): populate pricebook_price_cad/usd on new part insert

Coulton added pricebook_price_cad and pricebook_price_usd as NOT NULL
columns with no default to the parts table. The INSERT SQL omitted these
columns, causing a NotNullViolation crash on any new part.

- Add pricebook_price_cad/usd to INSERT column list and VALUES
- Compute as round(msrp * 1.1, 2) for new parts at creation time
- Use COALESCE in ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE to preserve existing pricebook
  prices and never overwrite them on subsequent BOM uploads
- Add migration SQL to backfill any existing NULL rows (run via PGAdmin)

Fixes #11

core: add Image class with pixel buffer management and basic operations

RGB24, and RGBA32 pixel formats. DWORD-aligned stride for Windows
bitmap compatibility.

Operations: fill, crop, rotate (CW90/CCW90/180), pad, blit, invert.
Format conversion between all pixel formats with configurable BW
threshold. 12 new test functions covering construction, pixel access,
all rotations, cropping, padding, format conversions, and deep copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix slow SIGTERM shutdown in creature-agent and creature-mqtt (2.19.4)

Both services were polling Task.isCancelled in a 1-second sleep loop,
but SIGTERM triggers graceful shutdown, not cancellation. The loops
never broke, forcing systemd to wait for its timeout and SIGKILL.
Replace with gracefulShutdown() for instant clean shutdown.

Log stderr when notary sidecar crashes to diagnose root cause

The notary binary's stderr was piped but never read. When the sidecar
crashed, we logged returncode=1 with no context. Now health_check()
captures up to 4KB of stderr and includes it in the log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Re-use content records in RemoteFeed to support pinning (#1673)

* Re-use content records in RemoteFeed to support pinning

Previously, any change to a remote feed item's data caused the old
content record to be deleted and a new one created. This broke pinned
content because the content ID changed on every update.

Now [type, name] is used as the stable identity for each item. On
refresh, matching records are updated in place (preserving their ID),
new records are created for items with no match, and records with no
corresponding item are removed. The digest is retained as a change
detector to skip unnecessary updates.

Graphic content stores its image_url in config so the image is only
re-downloaded when the URL actually changes.

Duplicate [type, name] pairs within a single response are handled
gracefully by appending a numbered suffix, making the identity stable
across repeated refreshes with the same duplicates.

Closes #1618

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify deduplicate_item_names using group_by/flat_map

commit.message:
fix(osint): add RFC 2822 date parser — reject articles with unparseable or old dates

Root cause: DateTime.tryParse only handles ISO 8601, but RSS feeds
use RFC 2822 format ("Wed, 08 Mar 2026 12:00:00 +0000"). Articles
with unparseable dates were assigned DateTime.now(), bypassing the
J/J-1 date gate and appearing as "today" in the feed.

Fix:
- Added _parseDateRobust() supporting RFC 2822, ISO 8601, and variants
- Handles timezone offsets (+0400, GMT, UTC, Z)
- If pubDate is empty → REJECT (return null)
- If pubDate cannot be parsed → REJECT (return null)
- No more silent passthrough of undated/old articles
- Cache key v2 → v3 (force fresh cache, purge stale data)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix globular cluster white-out sphere and open cluster invisibility

Globular: core particles were so dense with additive blending that all
channels saturated to white, creating a visible sphere. Added coreDim
factor that reduces brightness for innermost particles (r < 10% radius).

Open cluster: stars were invisible because (1) far plane 200K was too
short for 300K clusters, (2) star rendering size too small, (3) camera
started too far away. Extended far plane, tripled min visible size for
cluster stars, and moved camera 4× closer.

  to fix silent 0-result bug (per-league calls require season param)

  Middle East, "europe", "world" (fixes CL/EL/UECL trust-skip)
- football_data: COMP_CODE_COUNTRY map expanded with Scotland, Belgium, Turkey,
  Greece, Norway, Sweden, Denmark, Poland, Austria, Switzerland
- Fixed league ID bugs: 333=Ukraine, 848=UECL, added 40=Championship

commit.message:
Cash Flow: fix account columns, multi-select delete, auto-refresh, frequency picker

Bug fix: allocMap[a.account_id] → allocMap[a.id]; account objects from /accounts
use `.id`, not `.account_id`, so per-account salary columns were always showing —
and Upcoming Inflows was calculating correctly but display was broken.

Multi-select deletion: checkboxes + select-all in both salary and overlay tables;
"Delete Selected (N)" button appears in section headers when rows are checked.

Auto-refresh: page load now silently calls /cash-flow-overlays/generate and shows
a toast only when new CC/loan payment entries are created for the next 30 days.

Frequency picker (add mode): pill buttons for One-time / Weekly / Bi-weekly /
Semi-monthly / Monthly with live config (start date, day-of-month pickers, months
ahead). Auto-generates and previews the full list of pay dates before saving.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: active zones, HUD overflow, play tile, round guide restyle, leaderboard usernames

- admin-model.js: wrap vehicle_crossings clsData query in try/catch with .limit(2000)
  so a slow/failing query can't abort _renderIntel and zero out Active Zones
- stream.css: cap .ml-hud width on mobile (max-width: min(170px, 100vw-90px)) to
  prevent Vision HUD from overflowing off right edge of screen
- banners.js: make entire .bnr-tile-play clickable (not just Guess Now button);
  CTA gets stopPropagation to avoid double-fire
- markets.css: restyle round-guide from opaque box to minimal border-only accordion;
  removes background/padding, uses muted uppercase label, smaller toggle pill
- markets.js: round-guide collapsed by default on mobile (<600px) unless user has
  saved a pref; clicking anywhere on round-guide-head also toggles
- Supabase: created handle_new_user trigger to auto-create profiles on signup;
  backfilled all existing users (email prefix for real users, Guest+ID for anon)

Fix Qwen3.5-9B VLProcessor: unwrap text tokenizer for encode/decode

Qwen3.5-9B is multimodal — its processor lacks .encode(). Access the
underlying text tokenizer via getattr(tokenizer, "tokenizer", tokenizer).
